program_outcomes: İşletmelerin veri ihtiyaçlarını analiz eder, farklı veri tabanı yönetim sistemlerini değerlendirerek uygun veri tabanını tasarlar ve geliştirir.
İstatistiksel analiz, sayısal yöntemler, veri madenciliği ve makine öğrenmesi yöntemlerini uygulayarak veri kümelerini analiz eder ve karar destek çözümleri geliştirir.
Yapısal veya nesne yönelimli programlama yaklaşımlarını kullanarak web, mobil veya masaüstü platformlar için uygulamalar geliştirir
Kullanıcı merkezli tasarım ilkeleri doğrultusunda etkileşimli ve erişilebilir uygulama arayüzleri tasarlar ve geliştirir
İşletmelerin ürün veya çözüm geliştirme süreçlerinde kullanıcı gereksinimlerini belirler, önceliklindedir, planlar ve uygulanabilir çözümler geliştirir.
Farklı proje yönetim modellerini analiz eder, işletmenin yapısına ve proje türüne göre uygunluklarını değerlendirir, en uygun modeli seçerek proje yönetimi gerçekleştirir
Kurumsal stratejik hedeflere ulaşmak amacıyla bilişim kaynaklarını planlar, yönetir ve teknoloji yatırımlarının iş değerini artıracak bilişim çözümlerini uygular
Mesleki sorumluluk bilinci ile işletmelerdeki bilişim uygulamalarını etik ve toplumsal açıdan değerlendirir
İşletme fonksiyonlarına yönelik ihtiyaçları analiz eder ve çözüm alternatiflerini değerlendirir
Toplumun kültürel değerlerini ve tarihsel gelişimini analiz eder, farklı bağlamlarda sözlü ve yazılı iletişim becerilerini etkili, verimli ve olumlu biçimde kullanır
Derslerde edinilen bilgi ile iş ortamlarında deneyimlenen becerileri bütünleştirir ve çok disiplinli bakış açısıyla çözümler geliştirir

courses.name: Introduction to Economics I
courses.purpose: This course introduces the meaning and importance of economics and microeconomics, and helps students understand how economic reasoning is used to analyze markets and decision-making.
courses.description: This course introduces the meaning and importance of economics and microeconomics, as well as the use of mathematics and graphs in economics, supply and demand, elasticity, utility, and consumer preferences. The course also covers topics related to production organization, production, and costs, as well as market structures such as perfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic competition, and oligopoly.
learning_outcomes: Understand fundamental economic concepts such as opportunity cost, scarcity, and positive and normative economics.
Analyze the production possibilities of firms and countries, understand comparative advantages, and learn how to benefit from international trade.
Use the basic supply and demand model to understand the functioning of markets.
Learn how to calculate elasticity and how to use it in market analysis.
Analyze consumer behavior concerning consumption, saving, and employment.
Examine firms' decisions regarding pricing, output level, employment, and investment.
Will be able to analyze how firms behave in different market structures such as perfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic competition, and oligopoly.
Explore how to apply microeconomic concepts and principles in real life.

Hafta 14: Monopoly markets

courses.description: This course covers topics such as number sets, absolute value, inequalities, the concept of functions, basic functions, limits, continuity, derivatives, increasing-decreasing functions, the second derivative test, L'Hopital's rule, graph drawing, maximum-minimum problems, logarithmic and exponential functions, integrals and methods of integration.

Gain the skills to define functions and elementary transformations, draw their graphs, and perform applications.
Enhance students' ability to analyze linear, quadratic, polynomial, and rational functions and to solve problems using these equations.
Improve the ability to comprehend the properties of exponential and logarithmic functions, calculate compound interest, and perform related applications.
Understand the concepts of limits, continuity, derivatives, and asymptotes, and analyze the relationships among them.
Acquire the ability to calculate the derivatives of constant functions, sums, differences, products, and quotients using differentiation rules and perform marginal analysis.

courses.name: GENERAL MATHEMATICS II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the mathematical foundations needed to model and solve problems involving matrices, linear systems, determinants, multivariable functions, optimization, and linear programming.
courses.description: This course covers fundamental topics such as matrices, systems of linear equations, and determinants. The course also includes techniques like the Leontief method, linear inequalities, linear programming, the simplex method, minimization and maximization problems, multivariable functions, partial derivatives, maximum-minimum problems, Lagrange multipliers, and the least squares method.
learning_outcomes: Doğrusal denklem sistemlerini tanımlama ve çözme becerisi kazanarak, sistemlerin matris temsillerini oluşturma yeteneği geliştirmek.
Matrisler üzerinde Gauss-Jordan eliminasyon yöntemi ile işlem yapma ve sistemlerin çözümlerini elde etme becerisi kazanmak.
Determinant kavramını anlama ve Cramer kuralını kullanarak doğrusal denklem sistemlerini çözme becerisini geliştirmek.
Çok değişkenli fonksiyonları tanımlama, kısmi türevlerini hesaplama ve bu fonksiyonların maksimum ve minimum noktalarını belirleme yeteneği kazanmak.
En küçük kareler yöntemi ve Lagrange çarpanları yöntemini uygulayarak optimizasyon problemlerini çözme becerisini geliştirmek.
Doğrusal eşitsizlikleri tanıma, doğrusal programlama problemlerini formüle etme ve kısıtlı maksimum-minimum problemlerini simplex yöntemi ile çözme becerisi kazanmak.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO BUSINESS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the basic concepts of business management, the reasons for the existence of businesses, and their role in economic life. It also helps students understand business types, organizational structure, management functions, and expectations of business life after graduation.
courses.description: In this course, topics such as the meaning and importance of economics and macroeconomics, macroeconomics, national income calculations, aggregate demand and aggregate supply, employment and unemployment, capital, investment and savings, expenditure multipliers, fiscal policies, money and monetary policies, inflation, international trade, exchange rate regimes and financing of foreign trade are discussed.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic concepts of business management, the reasons for the existence of businesses, and their role in economic life.
Know the types of businesses; recognize and can analyze the internal and external environments of a business, and explains the factors affecting it.
Explain the stages of business establishment, forms of agreements, integration, and cooperation.
Explain management functions; understand organizational structure and the managerial process.
Learn about the expectations of business life after graduation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: General information about course
Hafta 2: Economic life and businesses
Hafta 3: Goals, revenues and expenditures of businesses
Hafta 4: Types of businesses
Hafta 5: Environment and environmental factors
Hafta 6: Environment and environmental factors
Hafta 7: Set-up process of businesses
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Feasibility studies
Hafta 10: Feasibility studies
Hafta 11: Mergers, integration and collaboration
Hafta 12: Management and organization
Hafta 13: Planning, organizing, directing, controlling
Hafta 14: Planning, organizing, directing, controlling
resources: Tuncer, D., Ayhan, D. ve Varoğlu, D. (2018). Genel İşletmecilik Bilgileri, Ankara: Siyasal Kitabevi.
Koçel, T. (2015). İşletme Yöneticiliği, İstanbul: Beta Yayınevi.

courses.name: Introduction to Algorithms
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to develop the ability to establish algorithms (flowcharts and pseudocode) by understanding the principles and phases necessary for solving any problem. It also aims to enable students to code the developed algorithm in the C programming language by using basic programming structures.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of algorithm design and programming, including input-output problems, decision-making problems, and loop-related problems. Students learn to develop solution algorithms, express them as flowcharts and pseudocode, and implement them in the C programming language.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental concepts of algorithm design and programming.
Develop an appropriate solution algorithm for input-output problems and code it using the C programming language.
Develop an appropriate solution algorithm for decision-making problems and code it using the C programming language.
Develop an appropriate solution algorithm for loop-related problems and code it using the C programming language.
Follow a written program and identify errors.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Computer Usage: Hardware and software features, computer structure, and functional parts and their roles: MIB-AMB, DB, memory, I/O units, secondary memory, data definition, bit, byte scales
Hafta 2: Introduction to Programming I: Steps in programming, definition of an algorithm, flowcharting, algorithm structures
Hafta 3: Introduction to Programming II: Solution algorithm design, design examples, flowchart and pseudocode design, testing the algorithm
Hafta 4: Programming Language: Basic elements of the C language, program structure, example with printf, C compiler setup, compiling, running, writing programs with C

Hafta 5: C Language Program Environment and Structure: main(), return, system pause, basic statements, comments, variables, constants, data types, simple arithmetic operations, assignment operations, and basic printf: First C program
Hafta 6: Input/Output and Basic Arithmetic Operations: printf/scanf
Hafta 7: Decision Making: Conditional selection, relational and logical operators, if statement
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Nested If and Multiple Conditions: switch/case structures, conditional selection, if/else, combined and nested if statements, ASCII code, getchar()
Hafta 10: Loop Algorithms: Loop structures and algorithm control, while, do-while
Hafta 11: Input Validation Using Loop Structures
Hafta 12: Loop Control Structures: Count-controlled iteration, counters, and for loops, loop structures
Hafta 13: Nested Loop Control Structure: Special counter operations, single increment/decrement and abbreviated operations
Hafta 14: Nested Loop Structures: Break and continue exit options for while, for, and do loops, with example
resources: Yorulmaz, M., & Yorulmaz, S. (2015). Programlamayı C ile Öğreniyorum. Palme Yayın Dağıtım. ISBN: 975-8982-51-6
Deitel, H. M., & Deitel, P. J. (2001). C: How to Program (International Edition). Prentice Hall. ISBN: 0-13-089572-5

courses.name: Introduction to Economics II
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of economics and macroeconomics and explains why they are important for understanding national and international economic activity. It aims to build students' ability to interpret macroeconomic indicators, policy tools, and economic relationships.
courses.description: This course covers key topics such as the meaning and importance of economics and macroeconomics, national income calculations, aggregate demand and supply, employment and unemployment, capital, investment, savings, expenditure multipliers, fiscal policies, money and monetary policies, inflation, international trade, exchange rate regimes, and financing foreign trade.
learning_outcomes: Define and calculate fundamental nominal and real macroeconomic variables.
Will be able to analyze the causes of short-term fluctuations in fundamental macroeconomic variables using the concepts of aggregate demand and aggregate supply.
Evaluate how changes in fiscal policies affect the national economy in the short and long term.
Explain the relationships between the central bank, the banking system, and money creation.
Understand the relationship between money supply, interest rates, and inflation rates, and analyze how changes in monetary policy affect fundamental macroeconomic variables.
Will be able to explain the factors determining a country's potential GDP, considering savings, investment, labor markets, and factors leading to increases in productivity.
Will be able to identify the variables affecting net exports and capital flows in exchange rates and analyze fundamental international macroeconomic relationships.

courses.name: Principles of Accounting I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental concepts and principles of accounting and help students understand how accounting information is produced and used in business decision-making.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts and topics of financial accounting, such as accounting principles, the accounting cycle, various types of accounts, the recording and tracking of accounts, and the preparation of balance sheets and income statements.

Understand the operation of the accounting system, types of accounts, principles of recording accounts, and their monitoring.

courses.name: ATATÜRK'S PRINCIPLES AND THE HIST. OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the principles of Atatürk and the history of the Turkish Revolution, and to help students understand the main concepts, events, and developments that shaped the foundation of the Turkish Republic.
courses.description: Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolution I course, outlined the qualifications of the Ottoman Empire, the internal and external reasons of the collapse of the Empire, the policies of the Western industrialized states against the Ottoman Empire in the 19th century also the national dimension of the war of independence and the development of compulsory causes of war are described in detail.
learning_outcomes: Explain Principles of Atatürk and main concepts related to Revolution history.
Explain the concepts of Reform/Revolution.
Describe the concept of National Forces.
Explain the concepts of Republic/Democracy.
Recognize the concept of Ideology.
Explain the main points of the period related to Turkish War of Independence and foundation of the Turkish State.
Explain the developments at Ottoman Empire before Turkish Revolution.
Describe the World War I and its results.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ataturk's Principles and History of Revolution objectives of teaching courses, Revolution and Revolution concepts, ideas about Atatürk, the Turkish Revolution, the main features of the Turkish Revolution.
Hafta 2: Internal and External Causes of the Collapse of the Ottoman Empire, Tanzimat and Constitutional Periods, Intellectual Movements in Constitutional Era
Hafta 3: Tripoli, Balkan, I. World Wars and the Armistice.
Hafta 4: The condition of the country against the occupation , Useful and Harmful Societies, The arrival of Mustafa Kemal to Samsun.
Hafta 5: M. Kemal's decision-National Struggle, Amasya Circular, Erzurum and Sivas Congresses.
Hafta 6: Amasya and Sivas Meeting, the Board of Representation to arrive in Ankara, The acceptance of National Pact.
Hafta 7: The opening of National Assembly , internal revolts, their causes and consequences.
Hafta 8: Turkish-Soviet relations, the Eastern Front, the Treaties of Moscow and Kars
Hafta 9: MIDTERM
Hafta 10: The Armenian Question, the Turkish-Armenian War and the Treaty of Gümrü
Hafta 11: South Side-Ankara Agreement, the Western Front-I. İnönü, the London Conference, II. İnönü, Eskişehir-Kütahya Wars.
Hafta 12: Battle of Sakarya, Mudanya Armistice.
Hafta 13: The Treaty of Lausanne
Hafta 14: Resolutions and Decisions of the Lausanne Treaty and evoluation of its decisions according to National Pact.

courses.name: TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain the importance of information for business and management, introduce the fundamental concepts of knowledge management, and present the process of knowledge management. It focuses on how information is created, retained, delivered, stored, and reused to enhance the intellectual capital of the business. Additionally, at a macro level, it aims to provide information on topics such as innovation, R&D management, and Intellectual Property Rights.
courses.description: This course covers the importance of information for business and management, the fundamental concepts of knowledge management, and the knowledge management process. It also addresses how information is created, retained, delivered, stored, and reused to increase the intellectual capital of the business. In addition, it includes topics such as innovation, R&D management, and intellectual property rights.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts of technology and knowledge management.
Know the importance of technology in management and have detailed knowledge about concepts such as R&D and innovation.
Understand the project preparation process and gains effective communication skills for presenting projects within businesses.
Gain knowledge about intellectual property rights and understands how businesses can acquire intellectual property rights.
Understand innovation process theories and has knowledge of innovation processes applicable within businesses.

courses.description: This course covers fundamental concepts such as variables, frequency distributions, graphs, measures of central tendency and variability, skewness and kurtosis, the basic elements of probability, including random variables, independent events, conditional probability, and Bayes' theorem. The course also addresses topics such as univariate and bivariate distributions, discrete and continuous probability distributions, the law of large numbers, and the central limit theorem.
learning_outcomes: Define populations, samples, variables, data, and data collection methods and effectively use these methods.
Create summary tables for qualitative and quantitative variables and analyze and interpret these tables.
Present visual information for qualitative and quantitative variables using appropriate graphs.
Calculate measures of central tendency and variability for quantitative variables.
Understand probability rules and perform probability calculations.
Carry out probability calculations for discrete and continuous random variables.
Develop analytical thinking skills.
Gain proficiency in using the SPSS program.

Hafta 2: Summarizing data obtained from qualitative and quantitative variables
Hafta 3: Graphs for data obtained from qualitative and quantitative variables
Hafta 4: Measures of central tendency
Hafta 5: Measures of central tendency, measures of variability
Hafta 6: Measures of variability, outliers, and box plot
Hafta 7: Problem solving
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Introduction to probability concepts, permutations, combinations, bivariate probability, conditional probability, Bayes' theorem
Hafta 10: Random variables, discrete probability distributions
Hafta 11: Some discrete probability distributions (uniform, binomial, Poisson)
Hafta 12: Continuous probability distributions
Hafta 13: Normal distribution
Hafta 14: Problem solving

courses.name: ATATÜRK'S PRINCIPLES AND THE HIST. OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION II
courses.purpose: Bu ders, yeni Türk Devleti'nin çağdaş bir kimlik kazanma çabalarını ve Atatürk ilkelerinin düşünce sistemi içindeki yerini öğrencilere kavratmayı amaçlar. Ayrıca Türkiye'nin iç ve dış politikasını tarihsel olaylar ve gelişmeler üzerinden neden-sonuç ilişkisi içinde değerlendirmeyi hedefler.
courses.description: Bu derste Atatürk dönemi, çok partili hayata geçiş denemeleri, Türk Devrimi karşı devrim hareketleri, Türkiye'deki anayasal gelişmeler, yeni Türk Devleti'nin oluşumu ve siyasi reformlar işlenir. Ayrıca hukuk, eğitim, kültür, sosyal ve ekonomik alandaki devrimler, 1932-1938 dönemi dış politikası, Atatürk sonrası Türkiye, II. Dünya Savaşı ve Atatürk ilkeleri ele alınır.
learning_outcomes: Atatürk ilkelerini ve önemini açıklar.
Avrupa ve dünyadaki gelişmelerin Türk Cumhuriyeti üzerindeki etkilerini açıklar.
Avrupa ve dünya siyasetinin Türkiye üzerindeki etkilerini ve sonuçlarını açıklar.
Kapitalizm/emperyalizmin Türkiye üzerindeki etkilerini açıklar.
Türkiye ile komşuları arasındaki ilişkileri/sorunları açıklar.
Türkiye'nin Avrupa ve dünya içindeki önemini açıklar.

Hafta 2: Türk Devrimi Karşı Devrim Hareketleri (Şeyh Said İsyanı, Takrir-i Sükun Kanunu, İstiklal Mahkemeleri'nin ikinci dönemi, İzmir suikastı, Menemen Olayı)
Hafta 3: Türkiye'de Anayasal Hareketler (1876 Anayasası, 1921 Teşkilat-ı Esasiye Kanunu, 1924 Anayasası, 1961 Anayasası, 1982 Anayasası)
Hafta 4: Yeni Türk Devleti'nin Oluşumu, Siyasi Reformlar (Saltanatın kaldırılması, Cumhuriyetin ilanı, Halifeliğin kaldırılması)
Hafta 5: Hukuk, eğitim ve kültür alanında devrim hareketleri (Tevhid-i Tedrisat Kanunu, Harf Devrimi, Türk Tarih ve Türk Dil Kurumları)
Hafta 6: Sosyal alanda devrim hareketleri (tekke ve zaviyelerin kapatılması, Şapka Devrimi, Soyadı Kanunu, Kadın Hakları, Uluslararası ölçü ve rakamların kabulü), ekonomik alanda devrim hareketleri
Hafta 7: Sosyal alanda devrim hareketleri (tekke ve zaviyelerin kapatılması, Şapka Devrimi, Soyadı Kanunu, Kadın Hakları, Uluslararası ölçü ve rakamların kabulü), ekonomik alanda devrim hareketleri

courses.name: PRINCIPLES OF ACCOUNTING II
courses.purpose: This course addresses the operation and valuation of accounts related to current and fixed assets, short-term and long-term liabilities, equity, and the income statement, in accordance with the uniform accounting system and chart of accounts applied in Turkiye. The primary goal of the course is to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary to prepare balance sheets and income statements.
courses.description: This course addresses the operation and valuation of accounts related to current and fixed assets, short-term and long-term liabilities, equity, and the income statement, in accordance with the uniform accounting system and chart of accounts applied in Turkiye. The primary goal of the course is to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary to prepare balance sheets and income statements.
learning_outcomes: Explain the definition, importance, fundamental concepts, and principles of accounting.
Gain knowledge about basic financial statements.
Learn the accounting cycle and its applications.
Acquire information about the operation of the accounting system, types of accounts, and the principles of recording accounts.
Recognize the Uniform Accounting System applied in Turkiye.
Understand the operation of fixed asset accounts.
Understand the operation of liability accounts.
Develop the necessary skills to prepare balance sheets and income statements.

courses.name: MATHEMATICAL STATISTICS II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop statistical analysis skills such as sample distributions, confidence intervals, hypothesis testing, and linear regression analysis.
courses.description: Review of binomial, Poisson, and normal distributions; convergence of binomial and Poisson distributions to normal distribution; distributions of sample means, proportions, and variances; point and interval estimates; confidence intervals for population means, proportions, and variances; hypothesis tests for single and two population parameters; correlation coefficient; linear regression analysis; general review.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental properties of binomial, Poisson, and normal distributions and their areas of application.
Describe convergence properties between different distributions and illustrate these properties with relevant examples.
Define the basic characteristics of sample and population distributions, analyzing the relationships between them.
Calculate point and interval estimates for data and interpret the meaning of these estimates.
Create confidence intervals by analyzing sample data and draw conclusions about population parameters based on these intervals.
Formulate hypothesis tests for single population parameters (mean, variance, proportion), select appropriate tests, apply them, and evaluate the results.
Formulate hypothesis tests for two population parameters (mean, variance, proportion), select appropriate tests, apply them, and interpret the results.
Calculate the correlation coefficient between data sets, conduct simple linear regression analysis, and interpret the results.
Use the SPSS program effectively in statistical analyses, manage the analysis processes, and evaluate the results.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Review of binomial, Poisson, and normal distributions
Hafta 2: Convergence of binomial and Poisson distributions to normal distribution
Hafta 3: Distributions of sample means
Hafta 4: Distributions of sample proportions and variances
Hafta 5: Point and interval estimates, confidence intervals for population means
Hafta 6: Confidence intervals for population proportions and variances
Hafta 7: Problem solving
Hafta 8: Midterm exam
Hafta 9: Hypothesis tests for single population means
Hafta 10: Hypothesis tests for single population variances and proportions
Hafta 11: Hypothesis tests and confidence intervals for two population means
Hafta 12: Hypothesis tests and confidence intervals for two population variances and proportions
Hafta 13: Correlation coefficient, linear regression analysis
Hafta 14: General review

courses.name: Organizational Behaviour
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the basic concepts and areas of organizational behavior and develop the ability to analyze and interpret human behavior in organizations.
courses.description: This course covers topics such as individual characteristics, behavior, and roles within organizations; group formation and behavior; learning and reinforcement theories; leadership approaches; decision-making methods for individuals and groups; power acquisition and usage; and interpersonal communication and conflict.
learning_outcomes: Comprehend the fundamental areas of behavioral sciences.
Analyze individual and group behavior.
Develop skills to enhance organizational performance.
Understand, control, and predict human behavior within organizations.
Reinforce theoretical knowledge through case studies.

Hafta 3: Individuals and personalities within organizations
Hafta 4: Individuals and personalities within organizations

courses.name: STRUCTURED PROGRAMMING II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students’ ability to design and code solution algorithms using strings and structures in C, and to introduce object-oriented programming concepts. It also prepares students to analyze and solve problems from an object-oriented perspective using C++.
courses.description: The course covers topics using the C programming language, including strings, string input and output operations, string arrays, character library functions, string input-output functions, string manipulation and comparison functions, structures, structure definition and access, file operations, data hierarchy, files and streams, basic concepts related to files, file types, file access modes, sequential access, opening, saving, reading, other file input-output functions, and an introduction to C++, including the concepts of classes and objects.
learning_outcomes: Develop an appropriate solution algorithm using strings and code it in the C programming language.
Create an appropriate solution algorithm using structures and code it in the C programming language.
Explain the concepts of Object-Oriented Programming.
Analyze and design problems from an object-oriented perspective and code them in the C++ programming language.
Solve problems using operator overloading and code them in the C++ programming language.

courses.name: CORPORATE FINANCE
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental concepts of financial management in market economies and to develop their ability to analyze capital sources, capital costs, securities valuation, financial statements, risk-return relations, capital budgeting, and working capital management.

Students will learn to identify sources of capital, understand capital costs, and evaluate securities while gaining knowledge and skills in financial statement analysis, risk-and-return analysis, capital budgeting, and working capital management.

Comprehend the cost of capital sources.
Evaluate the risks associated with capital structure.

courses.name: DATABASE MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to acquire knowledge and skills related to database design stages, database applications, SQL commands and the installation of database management systems.
courses.description: Course introduction and objectives, fundamental concepts, database approaches, introduction to database management systems. Database design stages, database requirements analysis and specification, verb-noun analysis, UML diagrams, data flow diagram. Conceptual design and entity-relationship modeling. Relational data models and transformations. Integrity constraints, relationship types, keys, constraints. Physical design, modeling, and implementation; MySQL installation, introduction and usage of "MySQL Workbench", data definition language (DDL) and SQL commands. CREATE and ALTER statements, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, SQL statements. SELECT and GROUP BY statements. SELECT LEFT JOIN, INNER JOIN statements. Case study and general review. Gain knowledge and skills at the levels of comprehension and application regarding the stages of the information system development life cycle and database design stages.
Acquire knowledge and skills at the comprehension and application levels regarding relational data models and data transformations.
Gain knowledge and skills at the levels of understanding and application regarding Sql, data definition language, data manipulation language, and related commands.
Acquire knowledge and cognitive skills at the level of comprehension regarding relationship types, keys, and constraints.
Gain basic knowledge and skills related to the MySQL Database; develops designs and applications using "MySQL Workbench" for solving database problems.

Acquire knowledge and skills to conduct a database design and application term project for solving a business-related problem, carrying out the necessary analysis, design, and application processes, and producing the required project outputs.

courses.name: HUMAN RESOURCES MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental concepts, functions, and processes in the field of Human Resource Management (HRM). All basic functions of HRM will be covered, ranging from HR planning to recruitment, training and development, and from performance management to compensation processes. Additionally, the impact of technology and digitalization on HRM will be examined, and information will be provided about the conceptual structure, purposes, benefits, limitations, and success factors of Human Resource Information Systems (HRIS). In this context, students will develop the skills to analyze the effectiveness of information systems used in HR practices and gain knowledge about current HR technologies.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts, functions, and processes in Human Resource Management (HRM). It includes HR planning, recruitment, training and development, performance management, compensation processes, and the impact of technology and digitalization on HRM. The course also addresses the conceptual structure, purposes, benefits, limitations, and success factors of Human Resource Information Systems (HRIS), as well as information systems used in HRM.
learning_outcomes: Explains the fundamental concepts, processes, and functions in the field of Human Resource Management (HRM) and gains the competence to apply them in the workplace.
Understands the importance and process of planning in HRM and develops the skills to create an effective human resources plan.
Explains recruitment processes and ensures effectiveness in candidate selection and evaluation.
Recognizes the necessary processes for employee training and development, conducts training needs analysis, and creates development plans.
Analyzes the impact of performance management and compensation systems on work efficiency and develops appropriate compensation strategies.
Understands how technology is used in HRM and its contributions to efficiency and effective management.
Analyzes the core functions of HRIS, its purposes, and the benefits it provides to businesses.
Recognizes the limitations of HRIS and the critical success factors for successful HRIS implementation, and can apply this knowledge in the workplace.
